126986.explanation: We are not in favor of a referendum. Furthermore, the council members are elected by the population and represent them as such.

126989.explanation: Entrepreneurship is free, but only if the environment agrees with it.

126990.explanation: The municipality will not engage in entrepreneurship.

126991.explanation: This is currently not an issue in the municipality.

126992.explanation: Good working conditions are extremely important to the PvdA. Employers have the duty to arrange this properly for their employees. It must not be the case that cheap labor works under poor conditions in our municipality.

127000.explanation: There are still enough undeveloped plots without agricultural designation. We prefer that housing remains concentrated in the centers rather than allowing building throughout our municipality. (An exception may be designating a larger area for homes rather than building detached houses scattered across our countryside.)

127002.explanation: The municipality and province currently contribute to the operation through a subsidy.

127009.explanation: We have a small municipality with not many residents, so we cannot house all facilities ourselves. We should focus on educating both victims and the community on how to recognize signals. It is also important that enough 'stay away from me' shelters are available in the region.

127011.explanation: Tourism is very important to us.

127012.explanation: In times of scarcity, as we have now, this should be allowed. It must be landscape-friendly.

127016.explanation: Many locations have already been designated, meeting the demand.

127017.explanation: Our municipality has a strong agricultural character. Roads designed for 30 km/h are not well accessible for these vehicles. However, we can focus on raising awareness of unsafe road situations caused by these vehicles. A recommended speed lower than the current limit can be requested of these road users without modifying the roads.

127018.explanation: You need care when you are in a vulnerable position. It is very important to us that people experience (some) control over their care. Care insurers must not determine this; however, we consider culture, religion, and beliefs less important in this matter.

127020.explanation: The residents of Westerwolde already pay the most in the province.

127025.explanation: Home seekers, including refugees, can register on a list of all rental homes in the province. Housing is allocated according to this list.

127026.explanation: Like in other municipalities, the polluter must pay for the disposal/removal of (bulk) waste. However, in our expansive municipality it remains a challenge to prevent illegal dumping in rural areas. But the good should not suffer because of the bad. It cannot be that we make this free for everyone because of that. Waste must be charged to residents on a cost-covering basis. That means everyone who produces little waste must contribute to those who do not want to pay.

127027.explanation: This could be possible if it fits within national frameworks and under certain conditions.

127028.explanation: Given the cost item, this should already be in place, but we do support swimming facilities in Vlagtwedde, though not at any price.

127029.explanation: There are already enough centers in the surrounding municipalities.

127030.explanation: In our water-rich country, no child should leave school without being able to swim. We have several swimming pools in our municipality, so for the municipality of Westerwolde this should not be a problem.

127031.explanation: This is very important for preserving our dialect.

127034.explanation: We are a large, expansive municipality. You want to prevent scattered properties being rented out as rooms because our municipality is less suited for that. However, we do believe that construction should take place for people who need (much) smaller and more affordable homes. This can then be done in places suitable for this type of housing rather than scattered throughout the municipality.

127035.explanation: Each village center has sufficient parking spaces.

127036.explanation: We have a large, expansive municipality. Many of our residents use their car for grocery shopping. It is not good for the activity in the center if we make it harder for our residents to reach the center. Additionally, public transport is not very well organized. So people have no alternative to reach the center. However, we must ensure safety and sufficient parking to make good use of everything Ter Apel has to offer.

127037.explanation: If the dispersion law is well enforced, it is likely that the nuisance from the so-called safe countries will decrease. There will always be space for asylum seekers to settle safely in our country. Additionally, the complex also provides us with a lot of employment. We want to maintain this, even after 2024.

127038.explanation: With our police station staffed 24 hours a day and the extra man-hours for the asylum complex, we are above the national standard. This must definitely be maintained. But further expansion is not necessary for now.

127039.explanation: We are a beautiful municipality but are not yet always well found. Investing in good tourist infrastructure is necessary to have a well-rounded offering of facilities within reach. The tourist of today is demanding. You cannot simply assume it will be fine. Additionally, this really offers many opportunities, also in the field of employment.

127040.explanation: This is under the province, they are responsible for this.

126986.explanation: We are not in favor of referenda at the municipal level. Decision-making requires careful consideration of interests, coherence, and long-term effects, for which the municipal council is elected. Good and timely participation of residents, however, we consider essential.

126989.explanation: Provided there is coherence with the surroundings. Farmers must be able to diversify their businesses with, for example, a shop or campsite. However, this must fit the character and capacity of the area, so that no one-sided stretches with only campsites or shops arise.

126990.explanation: Residents who jointly generate sustainable energy deserve support. This strengthens support, accelerates the energy transition, and keeps the proceeds local. Through the municipal sustainability fund, the municipality can concretely and effectively support such initiatives.

126991.explanation: Distribution centers take up a lot of space, cause extra traffic, and provide relatively little added value. We prefer to invest in other forms of business that better fit the environment and provide more employment and social value per hectare.

126992.explanation: Employers are indeed responsible for good and decent housing for seasonal workers, but this does not necessarily have to be on their own company grounds.

127000.explanation: Provided it fits within the municipal housing vision and provincial rules. In rural areas this can only be limited and careful, so that the rural character is preserved and undesired urbanization does not occur.

127002.explanation: The local broadcaster plays an important role, but additional structural funding beyond what the municipality already provides is not a given. The municipality must be cautious with extra expenses and allow room for a healthy, independent operation of the broadcaster.

127007.explanation: More visible community officers contribute to safety and livability in Westerwolde. Besides major safety issues, attention is also needed for minor annoyances, such as dog mess and nuisance. These precisely determine how pleasant people experience their neighborhood.

127009.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be helped quickly and safely. Good accessibility for residents of Westerwolde is essential, but this type of specialized facility fits better at the regional level. This way we ensure safety, expertise, and continuity of care.

127011.explanation: Permanent residence in vacation homes does not fit within provincial and municipal policy. We want to add homes in places where people really want to live. Only if no alternative demonstrably exists can temporary residence be considered.

127012.explanation: New construction outside existing village boundaries is not inherently undesirable. Adjacent to urban areas, this can in fact be necessary and appropriate, considering the housing task. Careful use of space and landscape integration remain key principles.

127014.explanation: The income limit for poverty arrangements must be carefully determined. Increasing it is not a given: support must reach those who really need it, while work and self-reliance are encouraged and arrangements remain affordable.

127016.explanation: More charging points for electric cars are needed. They support the energy transition, make electric driving more accessible, and meet growing demand.

127017.explanation: A general reduction to 30 km/h is not desirable. Traffic safety requires tailor-made solutions. In some places 30 km/h is appropriate, but on well-designed roads within built-up areas, 50 km/h is a fine and safe speed.

127018.explanation: Choice freedom in healthcare is important but cannot always be unlimited. The municipality must ensure accessible and quality care for everyone. Appropriate care is paramount, within what is practical and affordable.

127020.explanation: A reduction in property tax (ozb) is desirable but requires careful consideration. The municipality must be able to continue performing its tasks well. That means lower charges may have consequences for facilities and investments. Balance is essential.

127025.explanation: The municipality cannot completely refuse this. Under national legislation, municipalities have the task of housing status holders. However, we steer on pace, distribution, and customization so that this is done carefully and remains balanced with other housing seekers.

127026.explanation: Free disposal of bulk waste is not desirable. It causes extra costs ultimately paid by all residents. A reasonable contribution encourages responsible waste management and keeps the system affordable.

127027.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land are undesirable. Agricultural land is meant for food production and open landscapes. Sustainable energy should first be placed on roofs, yards, and industrial sites, respecting the character of our rural area.

127028.explanation: Closure of the De Barlage swimming pool is undesirable. Significant investments have been made and the pool is important for sport, health, and livability. We want to further develop De Barlage into a vibrant meeting place for residents.

127029.explanation: A new cultural center in Ter Apel is desirable. In connection with Nij Begun, this can grow into a strong meeting place for culture, associations, and residents, thereby contributing to livability and cohesion in the village.

127030.explanation: Structural funding of school swimming is not a municipal task. The choice for this we leave to the schools themselves. Swimming lessons are moreover well accessible in Westerwolde through our own swimming pools.

127031.explanation: Attention to the Gronings dialect is valuable for our identity. The implementation we leave to schools themselves. As a municipality, we organize and support this within our own framework, for example through the Grunneger Weke in Westerwolde.

127034.explanation: Allowing room rentals can contribute to better use of the existing housing stock. With clear rules, the municipality prevents nuisance and maintains livability, while creating more housing for those who need it.

127035.explanation: More parking spaces in village centers are desirable. They ensure accessibility, support facilities, and strengthen livability for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs, with attention to good public space design.

127036.explanation: A completely car-free center in Ter Apel is not desirable. Good accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors remains important. However, specific locations can be examined for traffic measures that improve safety and livability.

127037.explanation: We are basically pleased with the presence of the COA. In new agreements, safety and livability are prerequisites. The COA is an important employer and economic factor for Westerwolde.

127038.explanation: Westerwolde must urge the government for additional police capacity, given the special situation in the municipality. However, this must not come at the expense of regional deployment but should be supplementary.

127039.explanation: Investing in tourism is investing in Westerwolde. Our nature, culture, and tranquility offer ample opportunities. Targeted efforts strengthen the local economy, employment, and amenities, and will pay off in the long term.

127040.explanation: Active culling of wolves is not the municipality’s responsibility and is not currently necessary. There is no structural problem with wolves in Westerwolde. The wolf is protected; management and intervention are the responsibility of the national and provincial governments when safety or damage is at stake.

126986.explanation: The opinion of the residents is important.

126989.explanation: This is the citaslow philosophy: small-scale and authentic.

126990.explanation: The municipality must transition to sustainable energy sources and encourage local energy generation. We support the establishment and support of local energy cooperatives.

126991.explanation: There are distribution centers in neighboring municipalities. It is better to concentrate these.

126992.explanation: Housing for migrant workers should be provided in the municipality where they work. Their accommodations must also meet the SNF quality mark standards.

127000.explanation: Building only in accordance with the Residential Vision adopted by the council.

127002.explanation: Local news is important for community bonding.

127009.explanation: We support the goal (always providing shelter) but not the requirement for a fixed location. Safety and anonymity determine what works best; this is often better organized regionally. We do want quick access as well as good aftercare and guidance in Westerwolde.

127011.explanation: If a holiday park is no longer viable, other options for the holiday homes should be sought in consultation with the owners. Using them as permanent residences is certainly a good option.

127012.explanation: This is in line with the Residential Vision.

127014.explanation: Within the income limits set by the national government.

127016.explanation: More public charging points are needed so that residents without their own driveway (renters and those without private parking) can also drive electric. We want a good distribution across all villages, taking into account parking pressure and smart placement.

127017.explanation: Only if it is necessary for safety.

127018.explanation: In principle, it is good if people feel safe and understood in healthcare, and one's faith or beliefs may be part of that. At the same time, the municipality (and also healthcare providers) cannot guarantee this always: there is a shortage of staff, choice is not always available, and in emergencies or small villages often only one provider is available. We try to match where possible, but not always.

127020.explanation: Residents already pay many municipal taxes. This must be critically reviewed. The rate in Westerwolde is already higher than the national average.

127025.explanation: The municipality follows the task assignment from the national government.

127027.explanation: There are enough other alternatives to place solar panels, for example on roofs or as canopies over parking lots.

127028.explanation: It is a financial bottomless pit; the municipality should take the loss and divest the swimming pool.

127029.explanation: There are enough cultural centers in the nearby area.

127030.explanation: Not for school swimming, but the municipality can provide financial support through a participation pass.

127031.explanation: It is up to schools themselves to decide where to focus their attention besides the regular compulsory program.

127034.explanation: We are in favor because renting rooms provides additional living space without immediately building new housing. This can reduce pressure on the housing market and offers affordable places for young people and those who urgently need housing. A condition is good enforcement, minimum housing quality, and protection against exploitation.

127035.explanation: More parking spaces mean more car traffic and less space for a pleasant, safe village center. We want to make the centers greener and safer and tackle parking pressure with better layout, shared mobility, and good walking and cycling routes.

127036.explanation: This provides the opportunity for a beautiful boulevard by the water.

127037.explanation: Reception of refugees must be humane and manageable (dignified and manageable). Nuisance for neighbors must be prevented at all times; offenders must be dealt with more strictly.

127038.explanation: In a large, expansive rural municipality, police visibility and accessibility are important, but due to shortages, community officers are often deployed to other urgent tasks. We want more capacity so that community officers can truly do their neighborhood work (prevention, contact, early detection), together with enforcement officers, youth work, and care. Also less bureaucracy so officers can be out on the streets more instead of behind their desks.

127039.explanation: The municipality must make it easier for residents to enable small-scale overnight stays at home. This keeps rental income in the municipality instead of going to large project developers. And it remains an authentic experience for the tourist.

127040.explanation: We choose prevention and damage mitigation; good information, support for wolf-proof measures, quick handling of damage, and cooperation with the province and expert organizations in problem cases.

126986.explanation: The municipal council is elected to make decisions. Residents must be involved early in plans but ultimately the council decides.

126989.explanation: It strengthens the local economy. In addition, it offers consumers the chance to buy directly from the farmer.

126990.explanation: From GOUD Westerwolde, we want to support initiatives in the field of sustainability.

126991.explanation: There could be space for distribution centers on the newly developed business parks.

126992.explanation: We believe employers should indeed provide housing. This prevents seasonal workers from settling in our holiday parks that are intended for recreation and tourism.

127000.explanation: We choose to build within the villages.

127002.explanation: We support strong local broadcasters. They are essential for local democracy.

127007.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) must be visible throughout the municipality.

127009.explanation: A women's shelter is an expensive facility. We organize this together with other municipalities.

127011.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for tourism/recreation.

127012.explanation: We are proud of our rural area. No construction is allowed there. Within the centers, there is enough space to build new homes.

127014.explanation: We support the current poverty policy.

127016.explanation: There are more and more people with electric cars. Therefore, it is important to expand the number of charging points.

127017.explanation: For the different residential neighborhoods, we support a 30 km/h speed limit. But the main roads in a village should remain 50 km/h.

127018.explanation: For us, quality of care is decisive.

127020.explanation: Lowering the property tax (OZB) leads to budget cuts. However, we do think that the OZB must not rise more than the inflation rate in the coming years.

127025.explanation: A small portion of the available social rental homes is reserved for status holders. We comply with this assignment.

127026.explanation: Free disposal of bulky waste leads to an increase in waste disposal charges. This is paid by all residents, including those who do not bring bulky waste to the recycling center. We choose the motto 'the polluter pays.'

127027.explanation: There is no space for new solar parks in Westerwolde. In addition, we want to truly preserve agricultural land for agriculture.

127028.explanation: We want to keep our swimming pools open.

127029.explanation: From Nij Begun, there are opportunities to boost cultural facilities in the municipality. The location is yet to be determined.

127030.explanation: We find it important that all children have a swimming diploma. Therefore, we take good care of our swimming pools. For residents with low incomes, we have a scheme. Obtaining a swimming diploma is not a task for education.

127031.explanation: We consider supporting the Groninger language and culture important. We would like to see education pay attention to this as well. However, it is up to each school to decide on this.

127034.explanation: In our municipality, many people live in large homes. Renting out part of a home or a room helps to address the housing shortage.

127035.explanation: Many villages have parking problems. This causes dangerous situations. Therefore, more parking spaces will need to be created.

127036.explanation: For entrepreneurs in Ter Apel, it is important that people can park close to the shops.

127037.explanation: The contract with the COA (Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers) needs to be adjusted. This can be done in the coming period. The goal is to attract a different target group to the center.

127038.explanation: Westerwolde structurally has too few police officers, especially considering the size of the municipality. However, we should not only ask for more police; we must also address the causes of nuisance.

127039.explanation: We want to attract more tourists to the municipality.

127040.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

126986.explanation: D66 stands for democratic renewal and resident participation. A referendum gives Westerwolde residents direct influence on important decisions, thereby increasing trust and involvement in local democracy.

126989.explanation: From D66's perspective, it is good that farmers can try new ideas, such as a shop or campsite. But the municipality must also pay attention to safety, living environment, and nature. A fair process determines when a permit is appropriate.

126990.explanation: D66 believes it is important that residents can collectively generate sustainable energy. Municipal support helps them initiate projects and accelerates the transition to clean energy, which benefits the environment and society.

126991.explanation: D66 emphasizes finding a balance: distribution centers can offer jobs but also cause traffic pressure and environmental impact. The municipality must carefully weigh each case so that economy, living environment, and sustainability remain balanced.

126992.explanation: D66 stresses that employers in agriculture and horticulture must take responsibility for their workers. Good housing on their own premises ensures safety, well-being, and clarity for seasonal workers.

127000.explanation: D66 sees this as fitting with entrepreneurship and local development. Farmers can make their businesses more sustainable and future-proof while the municipality maintains control over spatial quality and living environment. It offers opportunities without fully relaxing the rules.

127002.explanation: D66 considers this consistent with promoting local engagement and independent information. Extra support helps the local broadcaster produce important news and cultural programs that inform and connect residents well.

127007.explanation: D66 believes this aligns with enhancing safety and livability in Westerwolde. More enforcement officers help maintain public order and give residents a sense of security, for example in Ter Apel, where some people experience insecurity.

127009.explanation: D66 holds that this supports protecting vulnerable people. A women's shelter offers safety and support for those experiencing violence so they can rebuild their lives with help and certainty.

127011.explanation: D66 considers this as tailored and smart use of space. There are many recreational homes in Westerwolde; by allowing these also as permanent residences, the municipality can reduce the housing shortage, provided a good balance remains between recreation and living for residents.

127012.explanation: D66 emphasizes that tailored approaches in housing construction are important. There must be various building opportunities to address the housing shortage and keep villages livable. If there are no serious objections, building outside village boundaries should be possible in a careful and sustainable way.

127014.explanation: D66 believes the municipality should make assistance accessible to everyone who needs it, even if that requires more flexible or higher income limits.

127016.explanation: D66 asserts this supports sustainability and clean mobility in Westerwolde. More parking spaces with charging points help residents and visitors of villages in rural areas to drive electric more easily, reduce emissions, and create opportunities for a more sustainable future.

127017.explanation: D66 advocates this for safety and livability. In most village centers in Westerwolde, this is already arranged. A maximum speed of 30 km/h reduces accidents, protects vulnerable road users, and creates a quieter and more pleasant living environment.

127018.explanation: D66 emphasizes this aligns with equal opportunities and self-determination in care. People must always be able to choose a care provider that fits their faith, culture, or beliefs, provided this is practically feasible and the care remains safe and of good quality.

127020.explanation: D66 states this helps ease the burden on residents. A lower property tax (OZB) makes living more affordable and ensures people have more money left for other important matters.

127025.explanation: D66 believes it is important that refugees with residence permits are housed quickly, but structural priority for social rental homes is not a sustainable solution and must not replace sufficient housing construction.

127026.explanation: From D66's perspective, we believe that disposing of bulky waste at the recycling center should be free of charge, to encourage sustainable and responsible waste behavior and prevent illegal dumping. However, it is important that this is well organized and misuse is prevented.

127027.explanation: D66 disagrees with banning solar panels on agricultural land. The energy transition requires careful choices and tailor-made solutions, where agriculture, landscape, and sustainable energy are balanced together.

127028.explanation: D66 opposes the closure of the swimming pool De Barlage. This issue has rightly carried heavy political weight in the past, precisely because it concerns not only costs but also livability, swimming safety, and the municipality's role in maintaining basic facilities.

127029.explanation: D66 opposes building a new cultural center in Ter Apel. Culture is important for livability and social interaction, but a new building should not be an end in itself. The municipality should first focus on strengthening and making better use of existing cultural facilities.

127030.explanation: D66 believes the municipality should allocate funds for school swimming. Swimming skills are essential for children's safety and contribute to equal opportunities regardless of home circumstances.

127031.explanation: D66 believes the municipality should enable primary schools to pay attention to the Gronings dialect. Regional language and culture enrich education and contribute to identity and connection, as long as schools maintain freedom in this.

127034.explanation: D66 believes renting out individual rooms should be allowed to alleviate the housing shortage, provided this is well-regulated and neighborhood livability remains guaranteed.

127035.explanation: D66 disagrees with this statement. Parking problems require tailored and smart solutions, not automatically more parking spaces. Livability and space for greenery and social interaction in our centers must remain paramount.

127036.explanation: D66 disagrees with making Ter Apel's center completely car-free. The livability of the center can be improved through smart design and more space for pedestrians and cyclists, but accessibility for residents, businesses, and visitors must be maintained.

127037.explanation: D66 disagrees with setting a fixed end date now for the asylum application center in Ter Apel. The reception of asylum seekers is a national responsibility and requires distribution and system improvement, not pushing problems into the future.

127038.explanation: D66 believes Westerwolde should urge the government for additional police capacity. Safety in our municipality is under pressure and requires sufficient visible and accessible police, tailored to Westerwolde’s specific situation.

127039.explanation: D66 believes investing in tourism can contribute to a strong local economy, provided it is sustainable and fits the character of Westerwolde. The quality of the area and livability for residents must always come first.

127040.explanation: D66 opposes actively combating wolves. The wolf is a protected species; problems require proper management, prevention, and support for livestock farmers, not eradication.

126986.explanation: A referendum does increase citizen involvement but should only be used for very important issues.

126989.explanation: This promotes mutual understanding and ensures risk spreading in the income of farming families.

126990.explanation: Generating sustainable energy is acceptable, provided it does not take place on good agricultural land. Preferably this should be done in cooperation within a neighborhood or village.

126991.explanation: Distribution centers are difficult to fit into Westerwolde’s landscape. Additionally, they cause many transport movements while generating relatively few jobs.

126992.explanation: Housing seasonal workers should be the responsibility of the entrepreneurs themselves. If this can be realized on their own property, it causes less nuisance and occupancy in recreation parks in our municipality. The holiday homes can then be used by tourists again.

127000.explanation: Provided it fits well into the landscape and does not impede neighboring farms.

127002.explanation: The local broadcaster is a commercial organization and should generate sufficient income independently. The municipality can contribute by involving the local broadcaster more and providing compensation.

127007.explanation: Good enforcement is important as it contributes to safety and livability. We have a large municipality with significant pressure around Ter Apel. The rest of the municipality must also feel safe. Both enforcement officers and more local police stations contribute to this.

127009.explanation: Costs are too high for the municipality to bear. Regional cooperation could be a solution or small-scale shelters. Even better is prevention and timely identification of problems. Safe places must be provided, but not in the form of a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

127011.explanation: Holiday homes should primarily remain available for tourists. Where parts of holiday parks have lost their recreational function, permanent residence may be allowed.

127012.explanation: Preference is for building within existing village centers. Agricultural land should not be unnecessarily sacrificed for housing. If there is local support for expansion, including from farmers, and it can be integrated into the landscape, expansion outside the village is an option. Offer tailor-made solutions.

127014.explanation: The number of households near or below the low-income threshold is above the national average, but people just above that threshold can still face financial problems without receiving support, which fosters debt and financial stress and leads to more problems.

127016.explanation: Charging stations where necessary, but no large-scale preferential locations. Residents should not feel 'forced' to drive electric vehicles as it is not suitable for everyone.

127017.explanation: Our municipality has many long roads within the built-up area. Adapting these to the 30 km/h standard is financially costly while safety there is adequate. However, consider the option of making roads in residential neighborhoods 30 km/h. Providing tailor-made solutions is a better option.

127018.explanation: Care and assistance come very close to people. It is important that residents can choose care providers with whom they feel comfortable.

127020.explanation: Lower property tax can help absorb rising costs such as energy and groceries for people with lower incomes. It also makes the municipality more attractive for small entrepreneurs. However, there is less money for facilities. We must see what is possible and do what is necessary.

127025.explanation: In times of housing shortage, the municipality has a responsibility to treat all residents in need of social rental housing equally.

127026.explanation: The costs for processing waste and bulky waste have increased sharply due to environmental legislation and rising gas prices. Free bulky waste disposal will lead to higher municipal taxes for all residents.

127027.explanation: More solar parks on valuable agricultural land is undesirable.

127028.explanation: For residents of Vlagtwedde and the region, this is an important facility. We believe the municipality should not have become the owner itself. We continue to focus on renovating and improving the swimming pool but also remain focused on future sale to a market party.

127029.explanation: We find a place where residents can come together very important. But instead of a new cultural center, we focus on multiple functions in one building and making use of existing buildings, such as the library.

127030.explanation: Being able to swim is a matter of life and death. Swimming lessons are intensive and costly for parents. School swimming is an excellent way to reach all children.

127031.explanation: The Gronings language is the language of our region. We see fewer and fewer people mastering the Gronings dialect. We can keep our dialect alive by paying attention to it in all schools.

127034.explanation: Room rentals can help create affordable housing and better utilize homes, but without clear rules, it can pressure livability, safety, and social cohesion in villages.

127035.explanation: More parking spaces are only needed where demand is structurally too high. To prevent the loss of green and playgrounds for children, we also focus on better design and behavioral measures.

127036.explanation: Ter Apel’s center has an important regional function. Visitors highly value parking right at or near the shops. A car-free center would negatively affect retailers and entrepreneurs.

127037.explanation: Many residents of our municipality work at the application center. They earn their salary there. Local entrepreneurs also do business with the center. We advocate for strict and enforceable conditions to be included in the new governance agreement with the COA.

127038.explanation: Sufficient police officers is a strict requirement. We too often see that too little capacity at the police not only compromises safety in and around Ter Apel but also impacts the safety of all our villages in the municipality.

127039.explanation: Tourism is an important source of income for entrepreneurs in our municipality. The municipality and entrepreneurs share responsibility for promoting tourism. The municipality can do more than just invest more money in tourism, such as easing regulatory pressure on entrepreneurs.

127040.explanation: Where wolves cause nuisance and display behavior that no longer fits natural wolf behavior (approaching people), the municipality must actively combat wolves to ensure safety for humans and animals.

statements.title: The municipality should actively combat the presence of wolves.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Westerwolde should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Westerwolde should urge the government to provide more police officers for the municipality.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: A women's shelter should be established in Westerwolde.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Westerwolde should close the De Barlage swimming pool in Vlagtwedde.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: Dropping off bulky waste at the recycling center should be free.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van grof vuil bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: Westerwolde should terminate the contract for the registration center for asylum seekers in Ter Apel by 2040.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The residents of Westerwolde should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Westerwolde moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The center of Ter Apel should become car-free.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to give attention to the Gronings dialect.
Westerwolde should build a new cultural center in Ter Apel.
The municipality should spend more money promoting tourism.
